personal.name: Shaurya Manjkhola
personal.tagline: BITS Goa 24–28 · ML enthusiast · Software developer · Competitive programmer
personal.bio: Third-year Mathematics and Computing undergraduate with a strong foundation in algorithms, machine learning, and software development. I approach problems methodically—breaking them down to core principles and building clean, efficient solutions. My work sits at the intersection of rigorous mathematics and practical computing.
personal.unique_edge: I combine a mathematician's structured, first-principles thinking with hands-on coding ability. Competitive programming has sharpened my reasoning under constraints, while ML and software development work let me move fluidly between theory and implementation.
personal.looking_for: Actively seeking internship opportunities in algorithms, machine learning, or software development.

projects.name: Transformer Architecture Benchmarking
projects.description: Benchmarked multiple attention mechanisms and positional encoding strategies to analyze their effect on language model performance and long-context generalization.
projects.role: Solo — designed experiments, implemented all attention variants, and built the benchmarking pipeline from scratch.
projects.problem_solved: Establishing fair, reproducible comparisons between diverse transformer design choices under identical training conditions.
projects.what_i_learned: The importance of controlled experimental design and how subtle architecture choices dramatically shift long-context generalization.
projects.challenge: Ensuring fair comparison across architectures by standardizing training configs and isolating variables systematically.
projects.impact: Produced a technical report documenting architecture trade-offs; framework is reusable for future ablation studies.

projects.name: Causal Uplift Modeling for Advertising
projects.description: Built a causal ML pipeline to estimate the true incremental impact of ad campaigns on customer behavior using interpretable Explainable Boosting Models (EBMs).
projects.role: Group project — contributed literature review, EDA, and feature engineering for the modeling pipeline.
projects.problem_solved: Separating causation from correlation in ad data to isolate true campaign lift rather than naive before/after comparisons.
projects.what_i_learned: How causal inference frameworks (uplift modeling) outperform traditional response prediction in measuring real campaign impact.
projects.challenge: Applying uplift modeling techniques to isolate true campaign lift instead of naive before/after comparisons.
projects.impact: Demonstrated measurable advantages of causal modeling over traditional response prediction with an end-to-end evaluation framework.

experience.description: Industry internship under BITS Pilani's PS-I program, receiving mentorship on AI applications and product development.
experience.contributions: Contributing to AI applications, software engineering initiatives, and collaborating with industry mentors to apply academic knowledge in real-world settings.

highlights: Strong foundation in algorithms and data structures
Advanced coursework in ML and statistical analysis

qa_seeds.question: What is Shaurya's background?
qa_seeds.answer: Shaurya is a first-year Mathematics and Computing undergraduate at BITS Pilani, Goa Campus (2024–2028). He has deep interests in machine learning, algorithms, and software development, and is actively seeking internship opportunities.

qa_seeds.question: What ML experience does Shaurya have?
qa_seeds.answer: Shaurya has hands-on ML experience including a comparative study of transformer architectures (attention mechanisms, positional encodings) and a causal uplift modeling project for advertising analytics using EBMs. He works with PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and Weights & Biases.

qa_seeds.question: What is Shaurya's competitive programming background?
qa_seeds.answer: Shaurya competes on Codeforces under the handle 'shaman.' with a current rating of 1437. He primarily solves problems in C++. Competitive programming has honed his algorithmic thinking and ability to work under time constraints.

qa_seeds.question: How can I contact Shaurya?
qa_seeds.answer: You can reach Shaurya at shaurya.manjkhola@gmail.com or call 8847499640. His GitHub handle is shaurya-manjkhola. He is based in Chandigarh, India, and open to relocation for the right opportunity.
